title: ESTIMATOR & PURCHASING MANAGER
description:
ESTIMATOR & PURCHASING MANAGER

Confidential — Luxury Residential Construction Company (Los Angeles, CA)

Bilingual English/Spanish Required



About Us

We are a family-owned luxury custom home builder based in Los Angeles, California. We build high-end homes in some of the most desirable neighborhoods in the city, with project values in the $1M–$3M+ range. We are growing fast — we currently have multiple active builds and a strong pipeline of upcoming projects.

We treat our team like family, we pay fairly, and we reward performance with bonuses. The right person here will grow with us for years — this is a long-term role, not a short-term gig.



The Role

You will be our Estimator & Purchasing Manager — a senior 2-in-1 role combining pre-construction estimating with full ownership of purchasing, vendor management, and subcontractor payment coordination.

You will touch every project from the 
nt it enters our pipeline through every dollar that flows out to vendors and subs. You will be the person who makes sure we never overpay, never miss a takeoff, always have samples ready for clients, and always have proper documentation before payments go out.

This is a senior-level role for someone who can read full architectural plan sets, perform accurate quantity takeoffs across all trades, build complete project budgets, and then turn around and manage every vendor and supplier relationship that fulfills those budgets.

Bilingual English/Spanish is mandatory. The majority of our subcontractors and field workers are native Spanish speakers. You will be communicating with them daily — by phone,
, and text. Native or near-native fluency in both languages is required.

You will be trusted with sensitive financial and project information. A signed NDA will be required before onboarding.



Work Schedule



Hours: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM Pacific Standard Time (PST), Monday through Friday (40 hours/week, with a 1-hour unpaid break)

For you in the Philippines: approximately 11:00 PM – 8:00 AM the following day (Manila time)

Why this schedule: You need real-time overlap with US vendors, suppliers, and subcontractors to chase quotes, follow up on orders, and coordinate payments. Most US vendor offices and our Spanish-speaking subs are reachable 8 AM – 5 PM PST.

Live overlap: Full overlap with our team in California and with US suppliers/vendors during their business hours.



This schedule is non-negotiable. We need someone whose body clock can adapt to a true overnight Manila shift long-term — please do not apply if you cannot commit to this.

We are also looking for someone willing to work additional hours when needed. Extra hours are paid, and strong performance is rewarded with bonuses.



What You Will Do

Estimating & Pre-Construction



Read complete architectural, structural, and MEP plan sets (30+ sheets per project) and understand what's being built

Perform full quantity takeoffs across all trades — concrete, framing, drywall, flooring, tile, plumbing fixtures, electrical, lighting, appliances, hardware, landscaping

Build complete project budgets from scratch using historical pricing data and current vendor quotes

Maintain and continuously update a master pricing database based on every project we complete (so future estimates get faster and more accurate)

Cross-reference every takeoff against the contractual scope of work to make sure nothing is missed and nothing is doubled up

Review subcontractor bids for completeness and accuracy — flag gaps, scope holes, and inflated pricing

Produce 70% complete draft proposals that the CEO finalizes and presents



Vendor & Supplier Management



Source and qualify new vendors across every trade and product category

Get competitive quotes (3+ per item for major purchases) and negotiate pricing

Build long-term relationships with key vendors to secure better pricing, priority delivery, and volume discounts as we scale

Negotiate free or discounted samples for client selections (tile, flooring, slabs, fixtures, hardware, paint, etc.)

Negotiate free product offerings whenever possible (showroom samples, discontinued items, contractor-program benefits)

Onboard new vendors properly — collect W9, Certificate of Insurance (COI), payment terms, and credit applications

Maintain a complete vendor database with contact info, pricing history, lead times, and reliability ratings



Subcontractor Communication (Spanish-Speaking)



Communicate daily with our Spanish-speaking subcontractors by phone,
, and WhatsApp/text

Translate scope, schedule, and payment communications between English (management) and Spanish (subs) accurately and professionally

Build trust and strong relationships with our subs so they prioritize our jobs

Collect quotes from Spanish-speaking subs and translate/summarize them in English for the CEO

Mediate scope or scheduling questions between the field PM and Spanish-speaking sub crews



Purchase Order & Procurement



Issue purchase orders for every material order across every active project

Track every order from PO issuance through delivery on site

Coordinate delivery dates with field PM and subcontractors so nothing arrives too early or too late

Manage returns, damaged goods, and replacement orders

Coordinate sample shipments to clients for selections meetings — track which samples have been requested, which have shipped, which have arrived

Maintain a complete sample library log per project



Subcontractor Payment Coordination



Receive subcontractor invoices and review for accuracy against the contracted scope and percent complete

Coordinate with the field Project Manager to verify that the work being billed is actually complete to the percent claimed (cross-reference with CompanyCam photos)

Once the PM approves work completion, package payment requests with full documentation: invoice, contract reference, percent complete verification, photos, and lien waiver

Submit payment packages to management (CEO) for final approval

Once management approves, coordinate with the office manager to prepare and deliver the check (subcontractor pickup, mailing, or wire transfer)

Communicate payment status updates to the sub directly in Spanish when needed

Update the AP log and project budget in real time for every payment released



Lien Waiver Management



Chase conditional lien waivers from every sub and supplier BEFORE payment is released

Chase unconditional lien waivers AFTER each payment clears

Maintain a master lien waiver tracker per job — no payment goes out without a conditional waiver in hand, and no project closes out without all unconditional waivers collected

Translate lien waiver requirements clearly to Spanish-speaking subs so there is never confusion

Flag any sub or supplier who refuses to provide waivers immediately



Reporting & Documentation



Send a daily end-of-shift summary
 to the CEO covering: (a) takeoffs completed, (b) quotes received, (c) POs issued, (d) deliveries scheduled, (e) payments processed, (f) lien waivers collected, (g) any issues or blockers

Send a weekly Friday report covering: pipeline estimates in progress, vendor pricing updates, payment summaries, and any cost overruns or savings identified

Keep all estimating files, vendor documents, POs, and payment records perfectly organized in Google Drive





Requirements



Bilingual English/Spanish (mandatory). Native or near-native fluency in BOTH languages — written and spoken. You will be tested in both languages during the interview, including a live phone-style conversation in Spanish. Do not apply if your Spanish is conversational only.

3+ years construction estimating experience. You must have prior experience as an estimator, quantity surveyor, or pre-construction coordinator on real construction projects — ideally residential. Tell us specifically what projects you've estimated.

Plan reading at a senior level. You must be able to open a 30+ sheet architectural plan set with structural, MEP, and architectural details and accurately quantify every material across every trade.

Quantity takeoff fluency. Experience with takeoff software (Bluebeam Revu, PlanSwift, On-Screen Takeoff, or similar) is required.

Purchasing or procurement experience. You should have experience managing vendor relationships, issuing POs, and coordinating deliveries.

Excellent written English AND Spanish. You will be sending dozens of
s per day in both languages. Your writing must read like a professional native speaker wrote it. We will test this in the interview.

Verbal English AND Spanish fluency. You will be on calls with US vendors and Spanish-speaking subs every day. Clear, confident spoken fluency in both is required.

Tech fluency. Bluebeam Revu (or equivalent), Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets, Gmail, Google Drive, WhatsApp, and ideally CompanyCam, Buildertrend, and QuickBooks Online.

Numerical accuracy. You will be handling money, quantities, and pricing data every day. One bad takeoff or one missed lien waiver costs us thousands. Mistakes are unacceptable.

Discretion and trustworthiness. You will see vendor pricing, contract values, payment information, and financial data. NDA required.

Reliable workspace. Stable high-speed internet, backup power, quiet workspace, working webcam, modern computer with two monitors strongly preferred.

Schedule commitment. You must be able to consistently work 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM PST (overnight in Manila) long-term. Confirm in your application that this works for you.

Available immediately. We are hiring for an immediate start.





Bonus Points



Experience estimating luxury or high-end residential construction

Experience with U.S. or California-specific construction (codes, lien laws, Title 24, prevailing wage if applicable)

Background in architecture, civil engineering, construction management, or quantity surveying

Experience working directly with Mexican or Central American construction crews

Familiarity with California lien waiver law (conditional vs. unconditional, progress vs. final)

Comfortable using AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.) to work faster





What We Offer



Competitive hourly pay (we will discuss based on experience — bilingual candidates with senior estimating experience earn at the top of our range)

Performance-based bonuses tied to: (a) project budgets coming in under estimate, (b) vendor savings negotiated, (c) on-time payment processing, (d) zero lien waiver issues per project

Long-term, stable role with a rapidly growing company

Real career growth — as our company scales, this role grows into a full Pre-Construction Manager position with team underneath you

Direct mentorship from the CEO

A respectful, family-owned culture that treats remote tea
mbers like real teammates — not like outsourced help





How to Apply

To be considered, your application must include all of the following:



A short paragraph in English explaining why this role fits you and why you can handle the 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM PST schedule (overnight in Manila) long-term

A short paragraph in Spanish describing your construction estimating background and how you would communicate with a subcontractor about a missing lien waiver

A list of construction projects you have estimated, your specific role, the project value, and the trades you covered (no client names needed)

A sample takeoff you have completed in the past (any project, any trade) — sanitized of confidential info

A short paragraph describing your process for getting competitive vendor quotes — how do you typically find vendors, get pricing, and negotiate?

A short paragraph describing one time you caught an estimating or pricing error before it cost a company money

Confirmation that you understand the schedule and can commit to it long-term

Your expected hourly rate in USD



Applications missing any of the above — especially the Spanish paragraph — will not be reviewed. We are looking for someone detail-oriented, numerically accurate, bilingual, and proactive — your application is the first test.



We look forward to meeting you and growing together.

title: CSR & Sales Assistant | $500–$900+ | High-Growth Team (PST Shift)

title: Product Creative Designer
description:
Job Summary:

We are seeking a highly creative and experienced Product Creative Designer to join our growing design team. In this role, you will be responsible for conceptualizing, ideating, sketching, and developing 3D models for innovative baby products that combine functionality, aesthetics, and emotional connection. You will drive early-stage product ideas — turning insights and trends into tangible concepts — and collaborate closely with our product and creative teams to bring these ideas to life.



This role requires strong conceptual thinking, aesthetic sensitivity, and technical product design skills. If you are passionate about designing purposeful baby products that make everyday parenting easier and more joyful, we would love to hear from you.



Note: Please see application instructions below.



Key Responsibilities:

• Conduct market and consumer research to identify trends, unmet needs, and product opportunities relevant to baby products.

• Generate creative and forward-thinking ideas through freehand sketching, moodboards, and visual ideation.

• Develop detailed 3D models and realistic renders that clearly communicate product form, ergonomics, and material intent.

• Collaborate with the Senior Designer, Creative, and Product Development teams to explore, refine, and finalize design directions.

• Translate abstract ideas and feedback into clear visual storytelling and refined design outputs.

• Support multiple design projects simultaneously, maintaining quality, accuracy, and alignment with brand standards.

• Use Photoshop, Illustrator, and 3D modeling tools (e.g., SolidWorks, Fusion 360) to visualize and present design concepts professionally.



Requirements:

• Minimum of 2 years of working experience in a relevant product design or industrial design role.

• A portfolio that highlights concept sketches, 3D visualizations, and final product outcomes — especially for consumer or baby-related products.

• Strong conceptual thinking — demonstrated ability to ideate and communicate design intent through clear hand sketches and storyboards.

title: Packaging Graphic Designer
description:
Job Summary:

We are a a young and dynamic growing E-commerce company focusing on baby and maternity products with customers based in the USA, Canada, and Europe. We are seeking a capable Packaging Graphic Designer to deliver positive results in packaging design. If you are looking for a challenging, rewarding, and fulfilling career, we welcome you to apply for the job below.



Key Responsibilities:

• Build packaging layouts on dielines (boxes, sleeves, labels, inserts, hang tags, stickers) using KeaBabies brand guidelines.

• Apply and format packaging content: approved copy, icons, benefit callouts, charts, barcodes/UPCs, product info, and required markings.

• Prepare print-ready files: correct bleed/safe zones, dieline alignment, CMYK setup, font handling (outline/package), linked images, and export specs.

• Create internal review mockups (clean 2D + simple 3D visuals) for quick approvals.

• Handle packaging updates and versioning quickly (pack count/size changes, language/copy updates, claim changes, retailer/Amazon variants, seasonal refreshes).

• Design occasional full-color packaging boxes for existing products (front/back/sides), keeping hierarchy clear and branding consistent.

• Maintain packaging templates and systems so new SKUs/variations stay consistent (type scale, grids, icon rules, layout logic).

• Organize and deliver final handoff packages to Product Development (clear file naming, folder structure, exports, and “final” version control).



Must-have requirements:

• Packaging design + production experience (consumer goods preferred), with ability to work from dielines.

• Strong understanding of print production basics:

1. bleed / safe zone / trim

2. CMYK vs RGB

3. exporting print-ready PDFs

4. font handling (outlining/packaging) + linked assets

• High attention to detail (typo-free, aligned layouts, consistent spacing, correct SKUs/info).

• Ability to execute both minimal / limited-ink e-commerce packaging, and full-color box designs for existing products when needed.

• Comfortable with fast iteration + versioning (counts, sizes, copy/claim updates, variants).

• Strong file hygiene: organized layers, naming conventions, clean exports, version control.

• Clear communication and ability to implement feedback quickly in an async remote workflow.

• Capability to use the following tools:

1. Adobe Illustrator

2. Adobe Photoshop + Acrobat



Nice-to-have, but optional:

• Working knowledge of InDesign

• Experience with Amazon/e-commerce packaging constraints (clarity-first layouts, compliance callouts)



Portfolio requirements:

• Portfolio showing packaging work (ideally includes at least: - one minimal/limited-ink style layout, and - one full-color box design)



Additional Information:

• Title your message: "I want to be your next Packaging Graphic Designer"

• No moonlighting is allowed during work hours in this role.

title: Tiktok Ads Scaler
description:
Smart — that's a direct AI honesty test. Adding it.

Here's the new section to include:

"7. Are you using AI to write this application? Just answer honestly with yes or no. I don't mind if you used AI to help — I just want honesty. Lying about it is an instant disqualification."

Why this works:



Most applicants are scared to admit AI use, so they'll lie

Honest people will say yes (and you can d
 if you mind)

Anyone who says "no" but the application clearly looks AI-generated = instant skip for dishonesty

Filters for integrity, which matters more than skill — you can train skill, you can't train honesty



Here's the FULL final job description with everything included:



I'm looking for a reliable, detail-oriented VA to help scale TikTok ad campaigns daily across multiple ad accounts.

You'll be working in TikTok Ads Manager and Business Center, helping me deploy proven winning creatives across many accounts to scale spend. This is NOT a creative role — I make the ads, you scale them. Clear SOPs and a Loom video walkthrough will be provided.

WHAT YOU'LL DO:



Duplicate winning campaigns across multiple ad accounts in Business Center

Verify the bid cap is set correctly ($0.19) before launching every single campaign

Upload Spark codes I provide to new campaigns

Monitor active campaigns and flag any that get banned or rejected

Update a tracking sheet daily with launches, spend, and performance

Pause underperforming campaigns based on simple rules I'll give you

Top up ad accounts when needed

Send daily updates on what was launched and results



WHAT I'M LOOKING FOR:



5+ hours daily availability, ideally full time

Strong written English

Reliable internet and a proper computer (not a phone or tablet)

Detail-oriented — able to follow SOPs exactly. Please include the word "pineapple" somewhere in your reply so I know you read this carefully.

Available on
 or 
 for communication

Long-term mindset — I want to build a real working relationship



GREEN FLAGS:



Previous experience with affiliate marketing or paid ads

Stayed at previous jobs for 2+ years

Available to start within the next week



WHAT I OFFER:



Competitive starting rate

Pay raises after 2 weeks if performing well

Clear training with Loom videos and written SOPs

Long-term position with growth potential as my business scales

Performance bonuses on profitable scaling



TO APPLY — IMPORTANT, READ CAREFULLY:



Start your message with the word "Bangkok"

Include the word "pineapple" somewhere in your message

DO NOT mention any specific platform experience (no TikTok, no
, no other ad platforms). I want to see if you can follow careful instructions, even counterintuitive ones.

Keep your entire reply under 100 words

Tell me your daily availability and timezone

End your message with the number [23] in brackets

Are you using AI to write this application? Answer honestly with yes or no. I don't mind if you used AI to help — I just want honesty. Lying about it is an instant disqualification.



I'll review applications and reach out within 48 hours.

title: Wedding Sales Planner
description:
We are a UK based street food and events company called Yum Street. We operate at major events, corporate functions and stadiums and are now expanding into premium weddings.

Our wedding offering is designed for high end clients who want something different. Instead of traditional catering, we bring multiple Airstream food units to create a unique and memorable guest experience. This is a new focus area for the business and we are building a dedicated pipeline of wedding enquiries.

This role is focused on helping us build relationships and generate opportunities within the wedding industry. This is not a basic admin role. You will be expected to think, research, communicate and contribute ideas.

Key Responsibilities

• Research and identify wedding planners across the UK including luxury planners, Asian wedding planners, marquee wedding specialists and planners who work on outdoor or estate weddings

• Research and build a database of wedding venues including country houses, estates, barns, dry hire venues, marquee venues and locations that allow external catering

• Research and identify other wedding suppliers such as florists, marquee companies, stylists, photographers and content creators who can introduce or refer business

• Build and maintain an organised CRM style database including contact details, social profiles, notes and follow up dates

• Carry out outreach to wedding planners, venues and suppliers using
, Instagram and LinkedIn

• Write personalised and thoughtful messages that build relationships rather than generic sales messages

• Follow up consistently with contacts and track all conversations

• Identify which venues allow external catering and which planners are most aligned with our offering

• Suggest new ideas and approaches to generate wedding leads and partnerships

• Assist with creating social media content ideas focused on weddings including Instagram posts, reels and story concepts

• Help position Yum Street as a premium and unique wedding supplier through messaging and content

• Monitor competitor activity in the wedding catering and mobile food space and report insights

• Support the creation of a wedding supplier pack by gathering information, ideas and content

• Track activity and results on a weekly basis including outreach, responses and opportunities generated

Key Experience Required

• Experience in lead generation, outreach or sales support

• Experience researching and building contact lists or databases

• Strong written English with the ability to write natural and engaging messages

• Experience using Instagram, LinkedIn or
 for outreach

• Comfortable using spreadsheets or CRM style systems to track work

• Ability to work independently and manage time effectively

• Experience working with UK or international clients is preferred

• Interest in weddings, events or hospitality is a strong advantage

• Basic content creation skills such as Canva or social media posting is useful but not essential

The Type of Person This Role Suits

• Someone who enjoys finding and connecting with people

• Someone who is naturally curious and willing to research beyond basic information

• Someone who is confident communicating and not afraid to reach out to new contacts

• Someone who takes ownership of their work and wants to contribute to results

• Someone who can think independently and suggest ideas rather than waiting for instructions

• Someone who is organised and consistent with follow ups

• Someone who wants to grow into a more senior sales or marketing role over time



This role is ideal for someone who wants to move beyond basic VA work and be part of building a real sales pipeline within a growing business

title: Junior Animator / Video Editor (Remote – Philippines)

We combine storytelling, animation, and AI tools to make learning fun, memorable, and accessible. Our platform is subscription-based, and we are focused on rapid growth through high-quality content and strong execution.



As an early tea
mber, you will work closely with a small, ambitious team and grow alongside the company.

This role focuses mainly on post-production and AI-assisted animation, not complex animation from scratch. You will use tools such as MagicLight AI and ChatGPT to efficiently produce short educational videos.

Stable, long-term work



Clear instructions and structure



Hands-on experience using modern AI tools in production

USD $400 per month



Full-time, remote

30-day trial period









Incentive & Growth Plan



In addition to the base salary:



The Contractor’s monthly base salary will increase by USD $10 for every 1,000 new paying members gained by VocabCartoons.



Salary increases are cumulative and reviewed periodically.



This incentive is designed to reward long-term contribution as the company scales.









Required Experience & Skills



Must-have



Minimum 6 months experience in animation and/or video editing



Good written and spoken English





Comfortable using:



AI tools (MagicLight AI, ChatGPT, etc.)



Basic video editing software (After Effects, Premiere Pro, CapCut, or similar)

Reliable internet connection and computer







 Nice-to-have (not required)



Experience with children’s or educational content



Basic subtitle timing and formatting experience



Simple motion graphics knowledge







Key Responsibilities





Animation & Video Assembly



Use MagicLight AI to generate animation scenes based on provided scripts



Assemble scenes into complete short videos



Ensure correct scene order and visual consistency



 Subtitles & Text







Use ChatGPT to:



Edit subtitles



Improve grammar and clarity



Ensure age-appropriate language



Sync subtitles accurately with audio





Post-Production



Add background music and simple sound effects



Add titles and on-screen text where required



Balance audio levels



Export videos in correct formats and resolutions







Final Delivery



Deliver clean, ready-to-publish videos



Follow file naming and folder structure



Apply small revisions when requested







What This Role Is NOT



To avoid confusion, this role does not include:



? Complex character design from scratch

This is an execution-focused role, not a senior creative position.



Performance Expectations

title: PRE-CONSTRUCTION DESIGN SPECIALIST & 3D VISUALIZER
description:
Confidential — Luxury Residential Construction Company (Los Angeles, CA)



About Us

We are a family-owned luxury custom home builder based in Los Angeles, California. We build high-end homes in some of the most desirable neighborhoods in the city, with project values in the $1M–$3M+ range. We are growing fast — we currently have multiple active builds and a strong pipeline of upcoming projects.

We treat our team like family, we pay fairly, and we reward performance with bonuses. The right person here will grow with us for years — this is a long-term role, not a short-term gig.



The Role

You will be our in-house Pre-Construction Design Specialist & 3D Visualizer — the creative right hand who works directly with our luxury clients to bring their vision to life before construction begins.

You will own the entire pre-construction design experience: building the client's needs and wants list, creating photorealistic 3D renderings of every space they want to visualize, helping them select fixtures and finishes that match their budget and the home's style, and producing concept designs for kitchens, bathrooms, closets, and cabinet layouts.

This role does NOT involve technical drafting or producing stamped construction drawings. Our licensed architect handles all final construction documentation. Your job is to design the vision, communicate it visually, and hand off a clear concept package to our architect.

This is a senior creative role for someone with strong design taste, polished 3D rendering skills, and the people skills to guide a $2M+ client through dozens of design decisions without overwhelming them.

You will be trusted with sensitive client information and design data. A signed NDA will be required before onboarding.



Work Schedule



Hours: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM Pacific Standard Time (PST), Monday through Friday (40 hours/week, with a 1-hour unpaid break)

For you in the Philippines: approximately 12:00 AM – 9:00 AM the following day (Manila time)

Why this schedule: You will be on Zoom calls with US clients regularly. Most luxury clients prefer late-morning or afternoon meetings. This schedule gives you full overlap with our team and our clients during their normal hours.

Live overlap: Full overlap with our team and clients in California during their workday.



This schedule is non-negotiable. We need someone whose body clock can adapt to a true overnight Manila shift long-term — please do not apply if you cannot commit to this.

We are also looking for someone willing to work additional hours when needed for urgent client deliverables. Extra hours are paid, and strong performance is rewarded with bonuses.



What You Will Do

Client Needs Discovery



Lead structured discovery sessions with new clients to capture their full needs and wants list — lifestyle, family, entertaining style, daily routines, must-haves, dealbreakers

Translate vague client wants ("I want a warm modern kitchen") into specific design directions with reference imagery

Build a per-client design brief that lives in our project file and guides every subsequent design decision

Maintain client mood boards on Pinterest, Milanote, or similar tools



3D Rendering & Visualization



Produce photorealistic 3D renderings of every space the client wants to visualize — kitchens, primary bathrooms, primary suite, family rooms, exteriors, pools, outdoor kitchens, etc.

Render multiple options for any given space (e.g., the same kitchen with 3 different countertop materials, 2 cabinet finishes, and 4 faucet options) so clients can compare visually before committing

Render to scale — every cabinet, fixture, and finish is dimensionally accurate so the client sees what they will actually get

Use SketchUp + V-Ray, Lumion, Enscape, 3ds Max, or Twinmotion to deliver photorealistic quality (not stylized)

Iterate quickly when clients request changes — a typical luxury client will want 4–8 rounds of revisions per space



Fixture, Finish & Material Selection



Help clients select countertops, tile, flooring, plumbing fixtures, lighting, hardware, paint colors, cabinetry styles, and appliance packages

Match selections to (a) the client's budget allowances, (b) their personal taste, (c) the home's architectural style, and (d) what's actually available and reasonable to source

Coordinate with our Estimator/Purchasing Manager to confirm vendor availability, lead times, and pricing for selected items

Build per-room selections sheets the client signs off on

Manage the full selections schedule per project — track what decisions are due by when so the project never gets held up by a missing selection



Concept Design



Produce conceptual layouts for kitchens, bathrooms, closets, mudrooms, laundry rooms, pantries, bars, and any custom millwork space

Design closet and cabinet layouts at the concept level — shelving, drawer placement, hanging rod heights, integrated lighting, hidden storage

Lay out furniture plans to confirm spaces work for the client's lifestyle

Produce concept floor plan adjustments when clients want to modify the architect's plans (e.g., "can we move this wall, expand this room") — communicate the concept clearly to our architect for final drafting and stamping

Generate elevation views and section views as needed to communicate concepts



Client Presentation & Handoff



Present design concepts to clients via Zoom — walk them through 3D renderings live, take notes on feedback, set expectations on next iteration

Build clean, polished presentation decks for every client meeting

Once a client signs off on a design package, produce a clean handoff document for our architect with all dimensions, specifications, fixture cut sheets, and design intent

Coordinate directly with our architect during their drafting phase to answer design questions and protect the client's vision



Documentation & Library Building



Maintain a master selections library — every fixture, finish, and material we have ever specified, with vendor info, pricing, and links

Build a 3D model library — reusable cabinet styles, fixture models, furniture, lighting that can be dropped into new projects

Organize all client design files in Google Drive — every project, every revision, every approval logged





Requirements



3+ years professional 3D rendering and interior design experience. You must have a portfolio of completed photorealistic renderings — residential interiors strongly preferred. We will review your portfolio in detail.

Photorealistic rendering software fluency. Required: SketchUp + V-Ray, OR Lumion, OR Enscape, OR 3ds Max + V-Ray, OR Twinmotion. You must be able to produce client-ready photorealistic renderings, not stylized 3D models.

Strong design taste. Your eye matters more than your technical skills. We will evaluate this through your portfolio. If your past work looks like a 1990s builder catalog, please do not apply.

Knowledge of luxury residential design. Familiarity with high-end materials, fixtures, and brands — Calacatta marble, Sub-Zero/Wolf, Waterworks, Ann Sacks, Visual Comfort, Holly Hunt, Restoration Hardware, etc.

Excellent written and verbal English. You will be on Zoom calls with luxury clients regularly. Your English must read and sound completely professional. We will test this in the interview.

Client-facing polish. You will be representing our company directly to clients spending $1M–$3M+. Your presentation, manner, and communication must reflect that.

Tech fluency. SketchUp, your rendering tool of choice, AutoCAD or Revit (basic), Photoshop, Adobe Illustrator or Figma, Google Drive, Google Sheets, Zoom, Pinterest, Milanote.

Detail orientation. You will be selecting hundreds of items per project. Mistakes (wrong fixture model, wrong finish code, wrong dimension) cost real money and damage client trust.

Discretion and trustworthiness. You will see private client information, design preferences, budgets, and home plans. NDA required.

Reliable workspace. Stable high-speed internet, backup power, quiet workspace, working webcam, modern computer with strong GPU for rendering, dual monitors strongly preferred.

Schedule commitment. You must be able to consistently work 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM PST (overnight in Manila) long-term. Confirm in your application that this works for you.

Available immediately. We are hiring for an immediate start.





Bonus Points



Background in interior design, architecture, or industrial design from an accredited program

Direct experience designing for U.S. luxury residential clients

Familiarity with California design trends (Modern Farmhouse, Spanish Revival, Coastal Modern, Mid-Century Modern, Contemporary)

Experience working with U.S. luxury vendors and showrooms

Photography or visual storytelling background (strengthens rendering composition and lighting skills)

Comfortable using AI tools (Midjourney, ChatGPT, Claude, etc.) to generate concept directions faster





What We Offer



Competitive hourly pay (we will discuss based on experience and portfolio strength — top-tier portfolios earn at the top of our range)

Performance-based bonuses tied to: (a) client design package sign-off speed, (b) revision rounds per project (fewer is better), (c) zero selections errors per project, (d) client satisfaction scores

Long-term, stable role with a rapidly growing company

Real career growth — as our company scales, this role grows into a Design Director position with junior designers underneath you

Direct mentorship from the CEO

Direct creative collaboration with luxury clients on multi-million-dollar custom homes — your portfolio will be loaded with high-end residential work

A respectful, family-owned culture that treats remote tea
mbers like real teammates — not like outsourced help





How to Apply

To be considered, your application must include all of the following:



A short paragraph explaining why this role fits you and why you can handle the 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM PST schedule (overnight in Manila) long-term

Your portfolio link (Behance, personal site, Issuu, PDF link, or Google Drive folder) — must include at least 5 photorealistic interior renderings you have personally produced

A list of luxury residential projects you have worked on — type of home, project value if known, your specific role, and the rendering software you used (no client names needed)

Identify 1 rendering in your portfolio that you are most proud of and explain in 3–4 sentences why — your design choices, your lighting, your materials, your composition

Identify 1 rendering you would do differently today if you could redo it, and explain what you'd change — we want to hire someone who keeps growing

A 1-paragraph mock client brief response: "A client wants a 'warm modern' primary bathroom in a Pacific Palisades coastal home, $80K finish budget, married couple in their 40s with two kids. What 3 questions would you ask them first, and what design direction would you initially propose?"

Confirmation that you understand the schedule and can commit to it long-term

Your expected hourly rate in USD



Applications missing any of the above — especially the portfolio link — will not be reviewed. We are looking for someone with both technical skill and real design taste. Your portfolio is the first test.



We look forward to seeing your work and growing together.

title: Industrial Spare Parts Procurement Specialist
description:
We are an international trading company based in Shanghai, specializing in sourcing niche European and American industrial spare parts for our clients. We work with brands like Parker, HYDAC, Norgren, Pepperl+Fuchs, Ebara, Eurotherm, Duff Norton, Sartorius, and many others.

Your role:



Research and find suppliers/distributors worldwide for hard-to-find industrial components (pumps, sensors, actuators, gas springs, controllers, etc.)

Contact manufacturers and authorized distributors to request quotes (RFQs)

Track down discontinued or obsolete parts through brokers, surplus dealers, and secondary markets

Compare prices, lead times, and payment terms across multiple suppliers

Maintain and update supplier database and sourcing progress in Google Sheets



Requirements:



Experience in procurement, sourcing, or purchasing (industrial/MRO parts preferred)

Strong research skills — you don't give up after the first "out of stock"

Comfortable cold
ing and cold-calling suppliers in English

Familiarity with platforms like Alibaba, eBay, EU Automation, Radwell, ThomasNet, and similar

Reliable internet, available for regular check-ins via chat



Nice to have:



Experience sourcing European or American industrial brands

Knowledge of MRO/spare parts market

Basic understanding of technical specifications (part numbers, datasheets)



This is a part-time role (20 hrs/week) with potential to grow into full-time for the right person. We value persistence and resourcefulness over speed.

title: Web Agency Project Manager - Part Time
description:
Role: Part-Time Project Manager (Web Agency)

Company: Dorset Tech (
 )

Working Hours: 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM UK Time (4pm - 8pm Manilla Time), Monday to Friday (20 hours per week)

Location: Remote



The Role

We are looking for an experienced, highly organised, and proactive Project Manager to join our remote team on a part-time basis. You will be taking over project management duties from our Operations Manager, ensuring that our web and app development projects run smoothly, stay on schedule, and exceed client expectations.



Because this role involves direct communication with our UK-based customers, exceptional English speaking and writing skills are absolutely essential. You will be the bridge between our talented development team and our clients, translating technical concepts into plain English and keeping everyone aligned.



Key Responsibilities

• Client Communication: Lead calls and meetings with UK customers to discuss project requirements, provide updates, and gather feedback.

• Project Tracking: Oversee the lifecycle of web design (WordPress, Laravel) and app development (Flutter) projects from inception to launch.

• Resource Coordination: Liaise with our designers, developers, and SEO specialists to ensure tasks are completed on time and to a high standard.

• Quality Assurance: Ensure that all deliverables meet Dorset Tech's rigorous quality standards before they are presented to the client.

• Process Improvement: Identify bottlenecks in our current project management workflows and suggest actionable improvements.



Requirements

• Agency Experience: Proven experience working as a Project Manager within a web design, digital marketing, or app development agency.

• Exceptional English: Great spoken and written English. You must be confident and articulate when speaking with UK clients on video or voice calls.

• Technical Understanding: A solid grasp of web technologies, particularly WordPress. Familiarity with Laravel, WooCommerce, or mobile app development is a significant advantage.

• Availability: Must be available to work from 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM UK time, Monday through Friday.

• Organisation: Great attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.

• Alignment with Our Values: A genuine interest in working for a transparent, community-focused, and environmentally conscious social enterprise.

You'll note that we're not asking for experience with a specific project management software - we have our own custom software and can adapt it to your specific preferences.



About Dorset Tech

We are Dorset's number one web design agency, and we are on a mission to disrupt the web design industry. We pride ourselves on offering the lowest rates in the country without compromising on quality, serving over 2,000 active support customers. We build everything from budget pay-monthly sites to full custom Laravel systems and Flutter mobile apps.



Beyond our technical expertise, we are a social enterprise and a carbon-negative company. We power our office with solar energy, drive electric cars, and plant a tree for every review we receive. If you want to work for a company that genuinely cares about its community and the planet, while delivering top-tier digital solutions, Dorset Tech is the place for you.



How to Apply

Please apply with your CV and a brief cover letter explaining your experience managing web development projects. Important: Please include a short audio or video recording if possible, introducing yourself, so we can assess your spoken English skills.



Important note: Please don't contact us direct about this job as you will not be considered - you must apply here.

title: Content curation assistant
description:
About the role

I'm a European footwear designer with 10 years of project work — shoe design, shoe development, material innovation, factory production, brand collaborations. I have a large archive of images and project material that has never been published. I'm building a portfolio on Instagram and LinkedIn, and I need someone to help me turn that archive into a steady stream of posts.

You are NOT creating content from scratch. The work already exists — hundreds of images per project. You're helping me surface the best ones, sort them, and prepare them for publishing.

This is a long-term role. I'm looking for someone to grow with over months, not a one-off project. The work starts heavier while we organise the archive together, then settles into a lighter weekly rhythm once the system is running.

What you'll do (starting task)

Your main job to start is image sorting and grouping. I'll give you project folders with 100–300 images each (sketches, renders, factory photos, prototypes, material swatches, travel shots, finished products). You go through the folder and sort the images into groups of 3–5 that go together visually or tell a small story. For each group, write me a short note: what you see in the images and what angle they could work for (e.g., "these show the shoe being made in the factory" or "these are close-ups of the material").

Once you're comfortable with that, the role expands to:

 • Image editing: Applying a preset filter to selected images in Lightroom (or a similar tool) so the whole feed looks consistent. This is batch work, not creative retouching.

 • Post assembly: For each group of images, you prepare a small package — the images, your note about what they show, and a short prompt for me so I know what to write about for the caption.

 • Scheduling: Loading approved posts into a scheduling tool (Buffer, Later, or similar). Setting the posting order and rhythm.



Your focus is purely visual

I handle writing, strategy, and community engagement separately. You won't need to write captions, create graphics from scratch, manage comments or DMs, or run ads. Your job is to make the archive look beautiful and organised.

What I'm looking for

 • A good eye for images — you can look at 50 photos and pick the 12 that stand out

 • Familiarity with Instagram and LinkedIn — you understand what looks good on a grid

 • Basic Lightroom skills (or similar app): applying presets, cropping, exporting

 • Organised — you'll work within a clear folder structure and keep things tidy

 • Good English communication — we'll check in weekly and work async the rest of the time

 • A background in communication, marketing, photography, styling or visual media is a plus but not required

Structure

 • Part-time: approximately 15–20 hours per week in months 1–2 (archive organising phase), then 4–8 hours per week ongoing

 • Flexible hours — schedule the work when it fits your calendar

 • One weekly video or voice check-in with me (I'm in Central European Time, UTC+1 — we can find a time that works for both of us)

 • The rest of the work is async: I give you a project folder, you return grouped images within a few days

 • Tools: Google Drive (shared folder), Lightroom, scheduling tool — to be decided together



Compensation

$450–$600/month depending on experience, with room to increase as the role grows. Payment monthly via Wise (TransferWise) or PayPal, your preference.

To apply

Send me two things:

 1 A short note about yourself — your experience and why this role interests you. Write it in your own words, don't list skills.

 2 A link to one Instagram account you think has a great visual grid, and tell me in 2–3 sentences what makes it work.

I'll respond to applications within a week. If we're a good fit, I'll send you a small test task with real images so we can both see how it goes before committing.

I look forward to hearing from you.

title: Performance Marketing Specialist
description:
Job Title: Performance Marketing Specialist



We are seeking a Performance Marketing Specialist to manage and optimize paid advertising campaigns across digital platforms. This role is suitable for someone who is analytical, detail-oriented, and experienced in improving campaign performance in a responsible and compliant manner.



Responsibilities:



Plan, set up, and manage advertising campaigns on platforms such as Google Ads and
 Ads

Monitor campaign performance and make data-based optimizations

Conduct keyword research, audience targeting, and basic competitor analysis

Manage budgets and ensure efficient use of advertising spend

Track key performance metrics and prepare regular reports

Run A/B tests to improve ad creatives and campaign effectiveness

Follow platform advertising policies and maintain account quality

Work with team members to support overall marketing objectives



Requirements:



Previous experience in digital advertising or performance marketing

Familiarity with platforms such as Google Ads and
 Ads

Basic understanding of campaign tracking and analytics tools (e.g., Google Analytics)

Ability to interpret data and make practical improvements

Strong attention to detail and organizational skills

Ability to work independently in a remote environment

Good English communication skills



Nice to Have:



Experience in industries such as e-commerce, lead generation, SaaS, or local services

Familiarity with conversion tracking or tagging tools

Experience supporting multiple campaigns or accounts



Application Instructions:

To apply, please provide:



A short summary of your experience in performance marketing or digital advertising

Platforms and types of campaigns you have worked on

Industries or niches you have experience in

Any examples of results or improvements (if available)

Tools you have used for tracking or reporting



We are looking for a responsible and reliable individual who can support campaign performance and contribute to ongoing improvements.

title: Direct Response Ad Script Writer + AI Voiceover

?

?

?

?

?

?

?

title: B2B Cold Caller — AI Automation Agency (US Market)
description:
Deyric is an AI automation agency that builds AI systems for car dealerships, med spas, and HVAC companies in the US. We need a cold caller to contact business owners from a provided lead list, deliver a short pitch, and book qualified meetings on our closer's calendar.

What You'll Do:

— Call 80–120 US business owners per day from a provided lead list

— Follow a proven script (we provide it)

— Qualify leads using 3–5 screening questions

— Book qualified meetings directly onto our sales calendar

— Log every call in our CRM

What counts as a qualified booking:

— Must be the business owner or decision maker

— Must be a car dealership, med spa, or HVAC/plumbing company

— Must confirm and show up to the scheduled call

Requirements:

— Proven B2B cold calling experience (minimum 1 year)

— Neutral or clear English accent — you'll be speaking with US business owners

— Comfortable with rejection and objection handling

— Reliable internet and a quiet workspace

— Available to start within 1 week

Earnings potential:

Base: $4/hr (approx $640/month full-time)

Bookings: $50 per qualified meeting

If you book just 2–3 meetings per week, you're earning $1,000–$1,200+/month

To apply: Send a 1–2 minute voice recording of yourself delivering this pitch: "Hi, this is [your name] calling from Deyric. We help [car dealerships/med spas/HVAC companies] capture more leads using AI-powered systems. I'd love to set up a quick 15-minute call with our team to show you how it works. Would Thursday or Friday work better for you?"

Put "DEYRIC" in your subject line so I know you read the full post.

title: GoHighLevel Email List Manager (GHL expert. Part-Time, Remote)
description:
We're a fast-moving health and wellness marketing team and looking for someone for one specific role here! 



We're looking for a GoHighLevel
 Manager who is detail-obsessed, constantly online, and takes mission-critical accuracy personally. This is a small but super important /mission-critical role! Mistakes here have consequences when we're sending an
 to thousands of people so "Wrong subject lines, wrong segments, wrong send times, major typos, and broken links" are not acceptable. 



We need someone who triple-checks everything before confirming, and then checks again.



It's a very simple but very critical piece here. This is a part-time role with a need for high availability. Expected response time during your work hours: within 1-2 hours max. When an
 is approved, we usually need it loaded same-day.





What you'll be doing:



- Upload the
 into Go High Level from a Google Doc

- Formatting copy within the
 designer of Go High Level (we have a template we need followed)

- Triple check to make sure the segment is sending to the right person

- Looking over the
 for any obvious spelling or grammar issues before it goes out

- Flagging anything that looks off and confirming with us before changing it. Some typos/grammar choices in our copy are intentional style choices, so always ask first.

- Excellent written English. You don't have to be a writer, but you should be able to spot when a sentence is genuinely broken vs. just stylistic.

- We will need you to be sending each
 with the proper UTM tracking links too.



Requirements:

- Detail-oriented to a fault. We want someone who triple checks the segment, the subject line, the preview text, the send time, and the from-name before hitting schedule. Every single time.

- Very present and active, and ideally flexible for urgent sends.

- Triple check to make sure there are no spelling or grammar mistakes that are glaringly obvious (we will sometimes send INTENTIONAL spelling/ grammar mistakes based on what we're saying)



You should ideally:

- Have a Grammarly account or some type of spell-checking software

- Have experience with GHL Smart Lists, Workflows, and the
 platform itself for one-off campaigns

- Organization capabilities within GHL





Bonus:

- You use Asana (Not a make or break, but it might make your life easier when managing some of the
 projects we have here).

- If you have a personal interest and love for all things health, detoxification, spirituality, holistic healing, and you are health-conscious yourself

- Background in
 marketing or working with online coaches and creators



Compensation: 

$5/hour

title: Social Media Clipper
description:
Job Title: Social Media Clipper (Short-Form Editor & Poster)

Platforms: TikTok, YouTube Shorts, Instagram Reels

Group to join: "
;

Term: Indefinite

Salary Range: Performance-based payout - agreement on PER VIEWS basis

Job Description: Social media clipper needed to cut long-form content (podcasts, livestreams, long-form videos) into high-retention vertical clips and post them across TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and Instagram Reels. Output: 15-25 approved clips per week. Must hook within the first 2 seconds, caption every frame (style-matched), and master platform-native pacing — no horizontal reuploads, no lazy jump cuts, no AI-voiceover slop. Fluent in CapCut or Premiere Pro, plus a caption/hook tool (Submagic, Opus Clip, or Veed). Pay structure: 1$ per 1,000 views on approved clips, scaling to 2$ per 1,000 once your 30-day rolling average hits 25,000 views per clip, and 3$ per 1,000 once it hits 50,000 views per clip. Minimum 10,000 views per clip to qualify for payout. Paid monthly. Required: portfolio of at least 5 previously posted clips with live links (no screenshots, no "trust me" submissions). Accounts must be yours or client-sanctioned — stolen or repost-farmed content = permanent ban and forfeited earnings.
